parsed_review.summary: CP00-300 is a descriptor-only RP09 (CRM/business-development) closeout pack covering 150 units across RP09.P01.M08.S06–RP09.P02.M07.S10. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is honest and well-supported: production_ready is false with status "implemented_pending_review" and pack_level_claude_review pending (no overclaim; the Hermes production_ready_candidate is a coverage-derived candidate, not a readiness assertion). Coverage/distribution constants in registry.js match the prompt's deliverable and micro-title distributions, sum to 150, span the exact range, and chain contiguously CP299→CP300→CP301; validators.js enforces these against the plan and tests assert coverage.valid===true. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced redundantly in service.js (freezeCp300Result pins all runtime/leak flags false, with base descriptor fields set before the row-extras spread so no extra can override them), in contracts/crm-core-contract.json, and in both test suites via deep-equality. Authority boundary is preserved everywhere (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true); Claude remains a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob) and Hermes is confined to deterministic H09 evidence-packet handling (no runtime receipts, not embedded in product). Review-hardening artifacts are valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1445-byte well-formed JSON, is_error=false), no forbidden tools, prompt excludes full source/diff, summary-only correctly prohibited, invalid_attempts_preserved empty. All pre-review validation commands (including rp05–rp09 cross-program regressions and git diff --check) report exit 0. Unrelated dirty files (.docx, new-session-handoff.md, LDIP c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt) are correctly excluded from active pack scope and were not attributed to this pack. Two P3 informational observations only; neither blocks pack or goal closeout, and there are no unresolved P0/P1 issues. Final enterprise-trust approval remains a human responsibility outside this receipt.

findings.title: Pack artifacts forward-referenced before post-review steps produce them
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts and every per-unit evidence_refs list cite docs/closeout-packs/cp00-300/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which do not exist at review time (only manifest.json and command-evidence.json are present in the pack dir). This is consistent with the documented hardened_review_sequence (baseline -> readiness -> read_only_claude_run -> normalize -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit), in which those three artifacts are produced by the steps that follow this read-only review. Pack status is implemented_pending_review and pack_level_claude_review.status is pending with valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[], so nothing is overclaimed.
findings.recommendation: No change required to the pack. Ensure the post-review normalize/adjudication/inspection steps actually emit all three referenced artifacts before npm run closeout-pack:validate and commit, so the forward references resolve.
findings.post_review_disposition: Verified expected and non-blocking; forward-declared artifacts are produced by the post-review steps after this review, and pack state honestly reflects pending review.

findings.title: Unused row-extras key in CRM_CORE_CP300_ROW_EXTRAS
findings.details: CRM_CORE_CP300_ROW_EXTRAS defines a documentation_entry key (and, via the CP299 spread, several model/scope keys) that do not correspond to any CP00-300 row title produced by crmCoreRowKey, so they are never merged into a constructed row. The lookup is a deliberate superset keyed by row key, and the reused CP299 model-row keys (e.g. ownership_metadata, reference_relationship_map, required_field_registry, package_directory_layout, primary_entity_identifier, tenant_scope_field, matter_trace_reference, lifecycle_status_enum) are genuinely consumed by the M08/M09/M10 sections. Base descriptor fields are applied before the spread and are not present in any extra, so there is no correctness, coverage, or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup: drop the unused documentation_entry extra (and any other dead keys) to reduce dead configuration. No functional change.
findings.post_review_disposition: Cosmetic/non-blocking; confirmed no base descriptor or no-leak flag is overridden by any extra and coverage validation is unaffected.
